displayTitle: Win the Ratio at the Coil

beats.narration: Every display shows two things: the signal you are paid to find, and the noise hiding it. The ratio between them decides the test.
The floor is three to one, and gain cannot rescue a poor ratio. Noise rides the coil before the amplifier, so gain lifts both together.
So name the enemy first. In the part, permeability variation is the loudest. From outside come power-line hum, welding arcs, cable pickup, and ground loops.
DC saturation cures it. Drive the steel onto the flat top of its B-H curve, incremental permeability falls toward one, and permeability noise vanishes.

payload.heading: Make the steel forget it is magnetic
payload.note: FLAT TOP → INCREMENTAL μ → 1 · NOISE OFF · δ RETURNS

beats.narration: Then ferrite shields the field, shielded cable blocks pickup, single-point grounding kills the loop. Prove three to one on the standard, then finalize frequency.

payload.sub: Win the ratio on the reference standard.
